notes: Canonical agent-agnostic model-switch-midsession cell for codex. Distinguishes from the codex-specific `model-switch` sibling by asserting MID-SESSION CONTINUITY as the load-bearing signal: one live codex rollout runs turn 1 on model A (gpt-5.4, the 0.130.0 boot default), the user invokes /model mid-session to switch to model B (gpt-5.3-codex) via the interactive two-step picker, then runs turn 2 on model B — all on the SAME rollout session_id from birth through teardown (every turn phase is pinned same_session_as session_birth, and no new_session is minted by the picker). The switch happens DURING the active session, between turns, with no observable state churn: the switch_window dwell phase asserts the session stays ready while the /model picker is open (the codex local-command picker fires no working/waiting transition), so the dashboard shows continuous mid-session activity rather than a restart. The model-name TRANSITION itself (A→B) is NOT expressible in the expected.jsonl phase DSL — the validator Phase struct (validate/expected.go) asserts only state/kind on the events.jsonl lifecycle stream, which carries no model_name field. The model change is captured at metrics level in summary.model_name of the .replay.json.golden (final value = model B = gpt-5.3-codex) and the per-turn turn_context.payload.model, which replay-fixtures.sh diffs. expected.jsonl therefore asserts the mid-session lifecycle skeleton + same-session continuity; the golden asserts the switch landed and TotalTokens accumulated across the two models.

text: Pre-session appears in ready within 1s of launch
Rollout session reconciles (different session_id), born ready — this is the one live session for the whole mid-session arc
PID bound to the rollout session
Turn 1 prompt (model A, gpt-5.4) → working on the rollout session
Turn 1 ends → back to ready; ModelName = model A (gpt-5.4)
User opens /model mid-session and switches A→B; the session stays ready throughout the picker window — no working/waiting flicker, no new session, same rollout still alive
After the mid-session /model switch to model B, turn 2 prompt → working on the SAME session
Turn 2 ends in ready on model B (gpt-5.3-codex); session never entered waiting; TotalTokens accumulates across the mid-session switch
Driver tears down the REPL — process_exited on the rollout session (same session_id from birth to teardown)
